tactical.rationale: The -17.7% drawdown puts LHX in legitimate setup territory for a defense prime compounder, but geopolitical backdrop remains supportive with sustained great power competition dynamics. Recent defense budget discourse around FY25 supplementals and early FY26 planning suggests continued DoD spending momentum. The technical setup is valid - RSI at 44.93 shows oversold conditions without capitulation, and the drawdown appears driven more by cohort rotation and rate sensitivity than fundamental deterioration. VIX at 16.76 suggests measured risk-off rather than panic, creating opportunity for quality compounders.

positional.rationale: Defense industrial base remains in structural expansion phase with bipartisan support for 3-5% real DoD budget growth through FY28. LHX's smallest-prime discount is creating relative value opportunity as capital rotates between defense names. The below-both MA position is concerning but typical for rate-sensitive defense compounders during Fed uncertainty periods. Aerojet Rocketdyne integration progressing well based on recent management commentary, with SRM duopoly positioning strengthening amid hypersonic and space propulsion demand acceleration. EPS revision trends have stabilized after Q3 supply chain headwinds.

strategic.rationale: The trailing P/E premium of +38.2% versus cohort is misleading - forward P/E estimates around 19-20x put LHX back within historical -5% to -10% discount range to larger primes. Book-to-bill metrics from recent earnings showed improvement to 1.08x with backlog growing mid-single digits YoY, indicating healthy demand trajectory. Operational margin pressure at 9.61% reflects temporary supply chain constraints and Aerojet integration costs rather than structural competitiveness issues. The yield at 1.6% remains sustainable within Dividend Aristocrat framework, though stretched compared to historical 2%+ levels.
det_notes: LHX P/E +38.2% vs cohort: premium — trim bias (rare)

composite.summary: LHX presents a quality defense prime compounder in tactical setup territory with -17.7% drawdown creating entry opportunity. While trailing P/E appears elevated, forward metrics suggest return to historical small-prime discount amid solid book-to-bill and backlog fundamentals.

Major F-35 mission systems or NGAD sixth-generation fighter program awards in coming quarters
Successful Aerojet margin integration with SRM pricing power demonstration
Ukraine and Taiwan supplemental funding packages driving accelerated ISR and missile defense orders
